purpose: Canonical active professional-skill catalog for the OPL-owned MAS Scholar Skills external enhancement pack and specialist Codex skills for MAS medical-paper writing, review, figure design, figure style, figure composition, literature, statistics, table, submission, medical data governance, and optional advanced or medical-method specialist workflows. MAS can discover these skill-backed capabilities through stage operating prompts, consume refs-only candidate outputs, package them through OPL Pack, prepare runtime context through OPL runtime env, and sync local Codex discovery copies through OPL Connect.
machine_boundary: This contract describes MAS Scholar Skills enhancement modules, syncable medical-paper professional specialist skills, optional specialist discovery skills, and their descriptor, dependency, run-context, artifact, receipt, and quality-evidence refs. It is not a generic OPL runtime base, not a MAS domain truth owner, and not a parallel default entry set outside medical-manuscript-writing, medical-manuscript-review, medical-figure-design, medical-figure-style, medical-figure-composer, medical-research-lit, medical-statistical-review, medical-table-design, medical-submission-prep, or medical-data-governance. MAS consumes the professional specialist skills from this repository and remains the runtime, study-truth, clinical data body, source-readiness verdict, artifact-authority, owner-receipt, typed-blocker, current-package, and publication-readiness authority. Optional specialist skills are refs-only helpers, not active modules or owner surfaces. This contract does not add an eleventh OPL brand module, execute domain stages, write domain truth, mutate clinical data bodies or artifact bodies, schedule runtime attempts, sign owner receipts, create typed blockers, write ledgers, or claim runtime, domain, quality, artifact, source, data, publication, App, or production readiness.

codex_skill_exposure_policy.tombstone_policy: opl-scholarskills and legacy optional specialist names have no SKILL.md, must not be installed by default, and may keep non-discoverable TOMBSTONE.md refs only.
codex_skill_exposure_policy.redirect_tombstone_policy: legacy named-specialty thin redirects are non-discoverable TOMBSTONE.md refs only; they must not expose SKILL.md metadata.

capability_module_classification_policy.optional_specialist_router_policy: default exposure remains false; optional exposure is named specialty only and points to module-level routers/reviewers before legacy redirects

dependency_profile_refs: opl runtime env prepare --domain mas-scholar-skills --profile display --platform <platform> --requirement-profile <path> --paper-root <path> --json
run_context_refs: opl runtime env run-context --domain mas-scholar-skills --profile display --json

display_quality_floor_policy.ai_executor_freedom: AI may choose the most suitable figure form, layout, panel hierarchy, and rendering backend from paper-local claims and evidence; this policy only raises the quality floor.

external_learning_refs.pattern: discoverable skill pack with provenance and topic-specific scientific communication skills
figure contract before plotting, backend-specific execution, and final visual QA
reference-driven retriever, planner, stylist, visualizer, and critic pipeline
multi-candidate academic illustration generation with critic rounds
reference style target plus reviewer preserve list to prevent drift
small hard plotting rules for vector export, color, size, typography, and parameter blocks
data profile, plot selection candidate, export lint, final-size grayscale preview, and programmatic-vs-AI visual review split as non-authority refs
claimType and graphWarnings for figure claim drift, annotation-to-source repair hints, and skill pack allowed-scope governance
data fidelity, claim-title truth, excluded-row handling, comparability, label economy, color-vision robustness, and render-then-verify checks as medical-figure-style refs
outline to panel render receipt to composite review loop as medical-figure-composer refs

scientific_figure_quality_floor_policy.ai_executor_freedom: AI may choose the most suitable figure form, layout, panel hierarchy, and rendering backend from paper-local claims and evidence; this policy only raises the quality floor.

source_patterns: Haojae/scipilot-figure-skill@43098ddb9e6a6d142218540c114f9ed38922fc42: data_profile_ref, plot_selection_candidate_ref, export_lint_ref, final_size_grayscale_preview_ref, programmatic_figure_audit_ref, ai_visual_review_ref, and route_back_hard_evidence_ref as refs-only hints

run_context_refs: opl runtime env run-context --domain mas-scholar-skills --profile tables --json

opl runtime env run-context --domain mas-scholar-skills --profile write --json

adapted_patterns: flag conclusion self-evaluation before owner gate
flag registry enrollment not-promoted prose before owner gate
flag MAS display-pack renderer prose before owner gate
flag TRIPOD boundary-citation prose before owner gate
flag defensible clinical story conclusion before owner gate
learned_pattern_policy.authority_boundary: refs_only_review_candidate_not_quality_verdict_or_reviewer_receipt; claimType, graphWarnings, annotation-to-source regeneration, workflow/tool-pipeline prose, conclusion self-evaluation, calendar enrollment period is not promoted, MAS display-pack renderer, TRIPOD is cited only as a boundary reference, defensible clinical story, formal analytic data-lock date remains, missing references, too-thin manuscript body, figure/table clinical-value gaps, figure-polish drift, registry descriptive scope drift, missing submission declarations, data-lock/enrollment ambiguity, diagnostic provenance gaps, figure-caption payload drift, blank PDF figure regions, double figure identifiers, unreadable wide main-text tables, nonmaterialized missingness atlas, missing adult-known-age BMI sensitivity tables, and uncompressed registry-atlas discussion themes are review hints only before owner gate

medical_sci_initial_draft_quality_floor_.registry_descriptive_boundary: Descriptive registry or atlas drafts may present cohort definition, availability, missingness, diagnostic ascertainment, subgroup distribution, and clinical interpretation candidates; they must route back claims that imply causal effects, population prevalence or burden, diagnostic prediction, treatment effect, or publication readiness without domain owner evidence.
medical_sci_initial_draft_quality_floor_.no_authority_policy: Scholar Review may emit review hints, verdict_candidate, route_back_candidate, and stop_or_continue_recommendation refs, but cannot claim MAS publication ready, owner accepted, typed blocker, current_package authority, quality verdict, or reviewer receipt.

run_context_refs: opl runtime env run-context --domain mas-scholar-skills --profile submit --json

publication_readiness_authority.reason: Scholar Submit may prepare refs-only submission candidates, but journal submission and publication readiness remain with the domain owner and human gate.

modules.legacy_module_id_policy: opl.scholarskills.data remains a legacy alias/provenance key only; active module id is mas-scholar-skills.data and active specialist work uses medical-data-governance
run_context_refs: opl runtime env run-context --domain mas-scholar-skills --profile data --json

sources.pattern: metadata scraping, paper library organization, tags/folders/notes, and export-oriented source metadata
raw materials, experimental logs, agent log aggregation, and structured manuscript input refs without becoming study truth
paper metadata, dataset/source manifest, run log, review summary, and resumable artifact bundle structure
systematic review protocol, inclusion/exclusion criteria, data extraction schema, and quality appraisal refs for SLR data handoff
dataset metric benchmark and result registry patterns as refs-only comparison inputs
annotation-to-source regeneration, project-local ledger pointer/hash, claimType graphWarnings, and skill pack governance as data/source repair hints

external_learning_module_fit.openscience_no_authority_boundary: OpenScience-derived refs cannot create source readiness verdict, owner receipt, typed blocker, publication verdict, artifact authority, current package authority, or second skill catalog

retention_closeout_policy.default_completed_project_policy: Prefer semantic reproducibility over byte preservation of historical process bodies. Preserve current cohort bodies hot; migrate useful historical information into semantic capsules; keep byte-level capsules only for active analysis, legal/regulatory retention, external handoff, or explicit owner decision; delete covered raw history and rebuildable caches.
retention_closeout_policy.no_authority_policy: Medical Data Governance may propose closeout refs and catalog shape, but cannot move, thin, delete, compact, publish, sign owner receipt, create typed blocker, or waive reproducibility.

medical_method_specialist_pack_policy.relationship_to_active_modules: medical-method router/reviewer skills are optional; they do not add active professional modules, do not replace the eight default medical-paper skills, and their absence does not block MAS ordinary progress
medical_method_specialist_pack_policy.codex_discovery_policy: optional medical-method work should start from module-level router/reviewer skills; legacy narrow specialist skills are retained only as thin redirect/tombstones
medical_method_specialist_pack_policy.missing_skill_policy: missing optional router or legacy redirect skill does not block the eight core modules or MAS ordinary progress
medical_method_specialist_pack_policy.default_core_skill_policy: default syncable skills remain medical-manuscript-writing, medical-manuscript-review, medical-figure-design, medical-figure-style, medical-figure-composer, medical-research-lit, medical-statistical-review, medical-table-design, medical-submission-prep, and medical-data-governance

advanced_specialist_pack_policy.relationship_to_active_modules: advanced biomedical routing is optional; it does not add active professional modules, does not replace the eight default medical-paper skills, and its absence does not block MAS ordinary progress
advanced_specialist_pack_policy.codex_discovery_policy: the optional advanced biomedical router is a real Codex skill with SKILL.md frontmatter; legacy advanced specialist skills are thin redirect/tombstones only
advanced_specialist_pack_policy.runtime_substrate_owner_policy: OPL_Runway_Connect_Fabric_or_consuming_workspace_compute_owner_owns_substrate_credentials_execution_and_harvest; optional skills own AI diagnostic playbooks and refs_only_candidate_handoff
advanced_specialist_pack_policy.missing_skill_policy: missing optional specialist skill does not block the eight core syncable skills or MAS ordinary progress
advanced_specialist_pack_policy.default_core_skill_policy: default syncable skills remain medical-manuscript-writing, medical-manuscript-review, medical-figure-design, medical-figure-style, medical-figure-composer, medical-research-lit, medical-statistical-review, medical-table-design, medical-submission-prep, and medical-data-governance; medical-figure-style and medical-figure-composer remain display subskills rather than active modules
